Resistant Discipline  

Description

1: Increase All Resists by 1 (L30) to 21 (L39)

Details   Raw Spell Data, Lucy Spell View

Mana: 0 Skill: Defense
Casting Time: Instant Recast Time: 1800
Fizzle Time: 0 Range: 200
Location: Any Time of Day: Any
Hate Generated: 1 Deletable: Yes
Blockable: Yes Focusable: Yes
Dispellable: No Interruptable: No
Short Buff Box: No Timer: 5
Target Type: Self Spell Type: Beneficial
Source: Live 12/03

Melee Flags

Initial Endurance Cost:0 Endurance Cost:3/second
Opening Types:
  • Triggered on demand

Messages

Cast on you: You channel your will into magical resistance.
Cast on other: Soandso has become more resistant.
Effect Fades: Your resistance fades.

Game Description

Focuses your will, increasing your resistances for a short time.

Items with this effect

  • Tome of Resistant Discipline

Quick Facts

Scroll:
Tome of Resistant Discipline
ClassLevel
MNK30
WAR30
BST51
BER30
ROG30
SHD51
BRD51
RNG51
PAL51
Expansion:

Duration:
5.0 mins
  • Comments (31)
  • Sold By (37)

Romida Truesword tells you, 'That'll be 6 platinum 6 gold 7 copper per Tome of Resistant Discipline.'

He's in Abysmal Sea with the rest of the tome people. Location is -207, -79, 99.

He also has:
Beastial Fury
Deftdance
Fearless
Holyforge
Leechcurse
Protective Spirit
Puretone
Sanctification
Trueshot
Unholy Aura
Weapon Shield

▲ Page top
Hot Keys
QuoteReply
# Jul 06 2005 at 8:04 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592256468217
77 posts
How do you make a hot key for the disciplines? I turned in the Tome to GM and cannot get a screen up that allows me to make a hot key. Looked through combat abilities and tried /disc got nothing.
▲ Page top
RE: Hot Keys
QuoteReply
# Sep 01 2005 at 3:45 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592759261266
Sage
52 posts
Use ALT+C to open your combat abilities window, then click on 'S' and the ability to hotkey the discipline. Then you can use it.
▲ Page top
its a tome
QuoteReply
# May 13 2005 at 10:17 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591818192577
5 posts
this isnt a spell its a tome look in the pok at the tome sellers and ul find this spell and lots of others u might need:)
▲ Page top
Tana Clawguard in POK
QuoteReply
# Jan 28 2005 at 3:27 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592759590858
Scholar
39 posts
Tana Clawgaurd has all 4 tomes. She is found in the library in POK. She only sells the Tomes. After getting it, you find a guildmaster and give it to him and he tells you to use your skill wisely. Then you bring up your combat abilities and make a hot key for it. As stated elsewhere, adds +10 to your Resists. Lasts a very short time, but maybe it's long enough to kill one Gnoll in Jagged Pine (SNICKER)

Edited, Fri Jan 28 15:29:41 2005

New Disc Usage
QuoteReply
# Jan 19 2004 at 5:29 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592251071989
289 posts
This discipline and fear and others are no longer linked so feel free to use it. The 10 bonus is still rather bogus though. Since it only lasts a short time and has a long refresh they could at least give you 50 or 100.

As for fear disc, well, if you raid a mob that fears you can use your discipline. Won't last long enough to get you through the whole fight but it will help.

# Dec 19 2003 at 3:36 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591795915318
1 post
This is a book you buy in the library in PoK (may be elsewhere as well) Enter and on the left side lower floor. Then turn it in to your Guildmaster. You can make a hot key to go in the new Combat Abilities window. =)

ound the vendor and bought the book for 2 copper. Turned it in to the Ranger Guildmaster in PoK and now I have my first disc.
▲ Page top
Info on Disciplines
QuoteReply
# Dec 16 2003 at 11:32 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592344490742
Scholar
65 posts
Yes, this is ONLY a discipline. Hybrids get this at level 51, pure melee get this at level 30. It seems SoE may be changing it with the new melee up grades. This raises ALL resists by 10 for *I believe* 2 1/2 minutes. This discipline is rather useless in my opinion. But if you have no others to use, or just fearless after this, then use it. Fearless is more useless than this one. I believe Fearless is lv 35 for pure melee and lv 54 for hybrids at the moment. Lord rorourke was right about typing /disc. Each pure melee/hybrid can do this in game, but depending on the discipline it can be used once every 4 minutes up to every 72 minutes. Resistant is a 72 minute timer that goes down as your level increases. Hybrids only have 4 disciplines at the moment, but hopefully everyone will get a nice upgrade to them. Also I believe that SoE is trying to set disciplines so they're on their own timer, not on each other's timer. This means you'll be able to burn every discipline you have right in a row, rather than taking time to choose which one you should use.

Sir Montug Starslayer <Warlords of Order and Chaos>
Level 64 Paladin of Tunare
Quellious Server
▲ Page top
RE: Info on Disciplines
QuoteReply

Malee Upgrades
QuoteReply
# Dec 07 2003 at 12:55 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591781922268
59 posts
SoE is publishing Malee upgrades. This is one of em. Others include a Warrior TOT ( taunt over time) these are abilities that you can use periodicly to increase your DPS,aggro,mitigation. you can use these like Bash,Taunt,kick that is already in use. these are to make us stronger in the face of our casting counterparts. Look forward to updating this so keep checkin back.

RE: confused
QuoteReply
# Dec 06 2003 at 5:34 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1616977257671
View User Forum
Scholar
102 posts
Not a spell listed on Casters Realm, not found on any AA window, possibly this is Allakhazam's way of listing the disciplines? If you type /disc in game you'll get a listing of various disciplines, one of which is called Resistant, though I had thought that it would have something to do with our Saves and not some sort of melee mitigation (what is melee mitigation anyway?). Can anyone help make sense of these? Thanks!

Edited, Sat Dec 6 05:38:34 2003
▲ Page top
RE: confused
QuoteReply
# Dec 07 2003 at 6:30 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593361685731
116 posts
Don't quote me on this, but I believe damage mitigation is lowering the amount of damage you take from successful hits. If you ever played that werewolf pen and paper rpg, it's like "soaking" damage. At least that's how I've had it explained to me.
